opsworks.instance.ebsblockdevice.DeleteO.Description: Whether the volume is deleted on instance termination.

opsworks.instance.ebsblockdevice.Iops.Description: The number of I/O operations per second (IOPS) that the volume supports. For more information, see [EbsBlockDevice](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_EbsBlockDevice.html).

opsworks.instance.ebsblockdevice.VolumeS.Description: The volume size, in GiB. For more information, see [EbsBlockDevice](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/API_EbsBlockDevice.html).

opsworks.instance.ebsblockdevice.VolumeT.Description: The volume type. `gp2` for General Purpose (SSD) volumes, `io1` for Provisioned IOPS (SSD) volumes, `st1` for Throughput Optimized hard disk drives (HDD), `sc1` for Cold HDD,and `standard` for Magnetic volumes.  If you specify the `io1` volume type, you must also specify a value for the `Iops` attribute. The maximum ratio of provisioned IOPS to requested volume size (in GiB) is 50:1. AWS uses the default volume size (in GiB) specified in the AMI attributes to set IOPS to 50 x (volume size).
